item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really appreciate the fact that there's a transcript despite the in-universe effects. Accessibility is ignored by a lot of format screws.

item.title: How clever!
item.content:encoded:
Congrats on another extremely clever and perfectly executed idea. I agree with Freddie AppsHero, this does have the feel of an AWCY piece. I Super High Fived the +1 upvote button!

(Additionally, how awesome is your haircut Miss McTiriss? Answer: Way Awesome)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was interesting, and quite a good format screw. Also, good acting there. Kate's voice is my new standard mental narrator for SCPs.

My only nitpick, because I'll always have one, is that as already pointed by some, it isn't really an infohazard. To me it is more like a cognitohazard, possibly a memetic one.

Congratulations, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just inserted a missing 'r' in 'researchers'. If somehow that was an intentional omission, my apologies.

Also, damn this is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how (as someone mentioned above) the Foundation can be made to do almost anything if it results in valid SCP documentation. It's like a cat chasing a laser pointer dot.

"Here, document this infohazard. The requirements are a practical joke, for all you know."

"Challenge Accepted."

And then they go do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent. Although people would probably have ended up grudgingly upvoting just for the effort and novelty of presentation, I'm glad that's not necessary, as the base SCP is legitimately odd and intriguing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't feel like AWCY to me, it feels like Andressen wanted to quit the Foundation, and found a way to make it almost impossible to discuss him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like a reference to AWCY would be appropriate. It's got their obsession with art, pranking people (especially the Foundation), and just general wackiness all over it. I'd also like to see an addendum or two about the missing person himself, and how the MTFs are working to find him, but I don't feel they are really necessary. It would just add a bit more to the story.

+1. Thanks for another good one McTiriss!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an interesting SCP,but could use something more I’m not sure what though.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 because this level of commitment is something to be celebrated. The requirements for the video were so….specific, and bizarre….I can't really think of anything else to say.

Oh, and the actress playing Alyx Romana was phenomenal—-great voice, as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. Pretty good actor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume the actor is Kate McTiriss, the author of the article. She didn't cite anybody else as the actor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How in the world did they figure out all the requirements?!

That is what is said somewhere in the collapsible:

The photographs pictured here, the quote by Oscar Milosz, a note, and a complex list of requirements that were eventually reduced to the eight listed previously, were anonymously delivered via post to Foundation diplomatic offices in Brussels on June 1, 2017.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent article, as usual, but, just wondering, who's the actor?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant! This made me chuckle heartily :D +1'd with glee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I know that. Idk what I was on about tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That isnt my skip, and it was in response to their comment about probably having the longest attribution list ¬_¬

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not to sound rude, but even tho I bet your SCP took a lot of work, I think Kate's is original because it's an actual video file while your videos are gifs. Good job both of you btw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How in the world did they figure out all the requirements?! Good new original SCP portrayal!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably meant to say that the mtf units are meant to watch the film.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fucking great, I love it. I'd +10 it if I could!
Unfortunately though, I think Bentu beat you out for longest attribution list not 4 days ago in SCP-3148 :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like things that baffle the foundation like this, especially when they have to resort to throwing Class Ds at it until it works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The film is to be screened all members of Mobile Task Forces assigned to investigations of both Vytas Andressen’s disappearance and the Slovenian National Society for the Preservation of Vakarinė’s Works, the group that has claimed responsibility for SCP-3972.

What should the first few words say?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even if I hated this, I'd feel compelled to upvote it just in recognition for all the work that went into it.

But I don't. I love me a good format screw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was incredibly silly, made very little sense, and ended up confusing the heck out of me.

+100.

(Seriously though, imagining someone intentionally manufacturing an SCP infohazard that requires the Foundation to go through so many hoops — effectively intentionally forcing the Foundation article to be a format screw — just for shits and giggles — is hilarious. And you know the Foundation would go through with it, too, because SCREW YOU WE LOG EVERYTHING NO MATTER HOW SILLY IT MAKES US LOOK)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nearly all photographic images of and true information regarding Andressen carries a Category Lambda-7 infohazardous effect.

Given that the effects can be avoided by communicating the information in a specific way, the SCP sounds more like it's memetic than infohazardous. Were people who already knew information about Andressen hit with the effects of the SCP once it became active?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like your hair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Good meta shit and nice short film. that's all i can say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this while eating popcorn, a first for myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because of a new original meta SCP that doesn't bother you with how meta it is. Also because the acting in the video was good.

Not only that but it's really surreal, sorry if I'm meant to understand what this means because I don't fully understand what the Dixieland Nightmare Magic canon is all about, but I really like the whole prank sorta deal going on.

All I can say? Really good, I love it.

1+

EDIT: Turns out this isn't related to Dixieland, really good job either way.

item.title: SCP-3972
item.content:encoded:
It is my ambition to be, as a private individual, abolished and voided from history, leaving it markless, no refuse save the printed books.
— William Faulkner

Someone’s missing, someone’s having fun, and one unprepared and uninformed researcher is in the middle of it all, watching D-classes throw up while watching her face.

There will be tales about this once I finish Dixieland Nightmare Magic, which is waiting on voiceover work.

What is surely the longest-to-make SCP I’ve published, and I’d think the longest attribution list in the site’s history.

+ show block
– hide block

Vytas Andressen:

  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/rebecca_harms/34712583031/
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/saeima/31311257782/
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/saeima/30648878723/
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/valstskanceleja/31343703001/
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/saeima/31459737815/

Lithuanian Landscapes:

  • https://flic.kr/p/6SU8EJ
  • https://flic.kr/p/6SUdxb
  • https://flic.kr/p/cwJidL
  • https://flic.kr/p/8gHuMR
  • https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I9GMUbX8Yts

Music:

  • https://archive.org/details/VrsAcoustics-64875-GabriellaBesanzoni

Quote:

  • Lithuanian poet Oscar Milosz’s 1929 ‘Symphony of September’

Portions of this film containing this photograph and this video are not allowed for commercial use. This film is not hosted on this site's servers and is in complete compliance with the rightsholders' licenses, but derivative works utilizing those two portions cannot be used in content hosted on this site.
